Traffic Treatment Policies

Override anomaly behavior. Most specific rule wins: IP (Highest) > Subnet > Alert Type > Global (Lowest).

Name Scope Priority Target Status Action

Select a policy from the list or click "Add Policy" to configure custom alerts tuning rules.

Rule Editor

Select a notification rule from the list or click "Add Rule" to configure target alert channels.

Available types: ddos, port_scan, traffic_spike.

Seconds to wait before sending another alert of the same type for this device.

Rule Explanation

Access Control Network Settings Collectors Setup Storage & Retention Detection Thresholds Notifications & Routing Integrations System Settings

Access Control

Update administrator security credentials

Must be at least 10 characters long.

Network Settings

Web server configurations and local IP subnets scope

Note: Modifying this port requires a daemon restart.

Collectors Setup

Telemetry collector ports and Suricata ingestion settings

Requires daemon restart.

Requires daemon restart.

Leave empty if Suricata telemetry is disabled.

Storage & Retention

Database storage backend and logs retention configuration

Requires daemon restart.

Requires daemon restart.

Pruning tasks execute dynamically every 24 hours.

Detection Thresholds

Global volumetric DDoS and flood trigger thresholds

Notifications & Routing

Outbound webhook credentials and Telegram configuration

Custom Webhook Headers (Authentication)

Add custom headers for webhook auth (e.g., Key: Authorization, Value: Bearer token123)

Integrations

Router exporter setup references and documentation

Integrate FlowGuard Lite with your edge networking equipment using the linked guides:

  • UniFi IPFIX/NetFlow Setup Guide
  • MikroTik Traffic-Flow Setup Guide
  • OPNsense / pfSense NetFlow Exporter Guide
  • Suricata EVE JSON Alert Guide

System Settings

Global debugging configs and logging levels

Set the local networks and collector ports for this node
